Chandler Parsons: I'm going to throw Minnesota out there just for fun. Chandler Parsons: I'm going to throw Minnesota out there just for fun. Amick: Yeah, that

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu open to returning to Chicago
item.description: Q. Have you ever been asked as a in consultant role or even just in conversation about how you would address what they should do next or what they should do first? Kendall Gill: No, I' I've never been asked those questions. Q. What would you tell them? Gill: I would tell them exactly what I just told you. And then I would also tell them that, and I know it's going to take a lot of money. I've discussed this with you guys as well. I know it's going to take a lot of money, but I would definitely t

item.title: Timberwolves have 'strong interest' in Derrick White
item.description: Similarly, league sources say the Minnesota Timberwolves — whose Antetokounmpo pursuit also appears to have cooled — have strong interest in the Celtics’ Derrick White.

item.title: Timberwolves interested in Josh Giddey
item.description: Darren Wolfson: Another player of interest to the Wolves, per multiple league contacts: Bulls guard Josh Giddey. Just not sure how you pry him out of Chicago, and I like his fit with new coach Tiago Splitter. #WolvesBack #Twolves

item.title: Jaylen Brown on Blazers' radar
item.description: Speaking of Plan Bs, league sources say the Portland Trail Blazers would have serious interest in Brown if the Celtics made him available. The Blazers have been among the suitors for Antetokounmpo since the February trade deadline but appear to be on the outside looking in at the moment. Also, league sources say the Minnesota Timberwolves — whose pursuit of Antetokounmpo also appears to have cooled — have strong interest in the Celtics’ Derrick White.

item.title: It would be incredibly difficult. But Connelly's …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: All of this from @sam_amick and @eric_nehm from last week still applies with the Wolves and Giannis. It would be incredibly difficult. But Connelly's aggressive mindset means you can't count them out while he's still available.

item.title: Celtics inquired about Rudy Gobert at the trade deadline
item.description: In reference to Minnesota's interest in White, which The Athletic's Sam Amick first reported Friday morning, trading him for Rudy Gobert would represent a clear salary match. Sources say that Boston, furthermore, has inquired about Gobert before ... most recently at February's trade deadline.

item.title: There's not been any indication that Giannis doesn't …
item.description: Sam Amick: I think the issue with Minnesota is that they have not been given any indication. There's two things. There's not been any indication that Giannis doesn't want to be there long term, but I don't get the sense that he's giving them the sign that he does want to be there. And that is another thing that as we get closer to draft day, I'm wondering with Giannis and his camp, are they going to step on the scale even more than they have to this point? Because right now, it's kind of a loose

item.title: Wolves looking to move up in the first round?
item.description: Philon’s workout is an indication the Wolves are canvassing their options in terms of moving up from No. 28 in the first round. The last time the Wolves moved up in the draft for a guard, they did so in a major way two years ago.

item.title: Wolves are set to play host to Alabama guard Labaron …
item.description: The Wolves are set to play host to Alabama guard Labaron Philon Jr. for an individual workout, a source told the Minnesota Star Tribune.

item.title: Wolves have gauged trade value of Julius Randle, Donte DiVincenzo, and first-rounder
item.description: The Timberwolves have gauged the trade value of Julius Randle, Donte DiVincenzo, and their No. 28 first-round pick in the draft, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu projected to earn $18-20 million per season in free agency
item.description: After trading for Ayo Dosunmu at the deadline, the Minnesota Timberwolves want to re-sign him. According to NBA executives who’ve spoken with HoopsHype, Dosunmu’s range is projected to be somewhere north of $18 million annually and could reach the low $20 million range.

item.title: Other teams have always called on rising swingman …
item.description: Other teams have always called on rising swingman Jaden McDaniels. Minnesota has made him essentially untradable unless it was for a blockbuster trade involving Giannis Antetokounmpo or Nikola Jokic. When the Los Angeles Clippers were struggling earlier this season amid a 6-21 start and gauging the trade market for Kawhi Leonard and James Harden each, the Clippers poked around on McDaniels, but were rebuffed, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the …
item.description: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the Phoenix Suns, Dallas Mavericks, Minnesota Timberwolves, Boston Celtics, and Cleveland Cavaliers, sources said, ahead of the NBA Draft, which takes place over June 24 and 25 (AEDT) at the Barclays Centre in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: Wolves keeping Jaden McDaniels?
item.description: The Wolves have been holding firm on saying no on McDaniels, team sources said. Center Joan Beringer is also a player the Wolves want to keep because of his size, athleticism and feel for the game. What the Wolves do not want to do is liquidate their depth in making a trade to pair the 31-year-old Antetokounmpo with Edwards.

item.title: One team that shall remain nameless to protect the …
item.description: For instance, team sources say one team that shall remain nameless to protect the shameless called the Timberwolves and offered a sparingly used bench player averaging single-digit points for Reid.

item.title: source said Philon performed very well in the workout
item.description: A team source said Philon performed very well in the workout on Saturday, but this is the kind of thing that feels almost a little too obvious. Why would Philon come to Minnesota this late in the process if he didn’t think the Wolves would be in a position to draft him? And why would it be out there in big, bold letters for the rest of the NBA to see? It is all very curious.

item.title: The Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent …
item.description: Is Minnesota still in the hunt? Not as of Friday. That's when The Stein Line revealed in the piece below that the Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent Antetokounmpo trade suitors in February, quietly "circled back to the Bucks this week to see what it would take to make another run at pairing Antetokounmpo with Anthony Edwards." Only for the Wolves to be informed, league sources say, that the Bucks want "even more now than the teams discussed in the winter."

item.title: Rudy Gobert: 'I'm still very far from thinking about retirement'
item.description: Rudy Gobert: "I'm still very far from retirement, I'm still very far from thinking about retirement. I'm still, I visualize a bit of the years that are coming and how I see the future, but but I'm really focused also on the present moment and when it will be the end of my, of my career, whether it's in the French team or in the NBA, I feel super well. I'm coming out of a semi-final of conference."

item.title: It's true that these big media in addition tend to …
item.description: Rudy Gobert: "Unfortunately, today the opinion of the majority of fans is based on that in fact. It's based on what when they go on Instagram, they see videos and they see what the big media say, and I, it's true that these big media in addition tend to show more negative things than positive things about me. So necessarily, I am not surprised."

item.title: Wolves hot for Derrick White?
item.description: Minnesota has been most aggressive in pursuing White, the Celtics’ defensive mastermind who has the experience and the savvy to help Edwards and the Wolves break through in the West. White turns 32 next month and saw his 3-point shooting percentage drop considerably this season, but so far the Celtics have rebuffed the Wolves’ advances.

item.title: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis Antetokounmpo thing at different points. They're not involved right now. What happens there? They know they need to make an upgrade, they know they need to add something to this team with Anthony Edwards on it. They've made a pitch on a bunch of different guys around the league. We'll see who they can come up with, but there's going to be a lot of tentacles that come out."

item.title: Julius Randle heads to Brooklyn in three-team trade
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Minnesota is sending Julius Randle and the No. 28 pick in the NBA Draft to the Brooklyn Nets in a three-team trade that sends Nic Claxton to the Chicago Bulls, sources tell ESPN. The Timberwolves will acquire Brooklyn’s No. 33 pick for Randle and No. 28.

item.title: Timberwolves are trading Julius Randle to Brooklyn,
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Timberwolves are trading Julius Randle to Brooklyn, sources say

item.title: Timberwolves free up significant salary with the …
item.description: Shams Charania: Timberwolves free up significant salary with the remaining two years of Randle's deal, giving them more flexibility to retain Ayo Dosunmu and use exceptions. Nets acquire a three-time All-Star and two-time All-NBA forward in Randle into their cap space -- plus a first-rounder. Chicago uses part of its space on a talented athletic center, a position of need, with $30 million in room left.

item.title: Timberwolves now have the flexibility to re-sign Ayo …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The Timberwolves now have the flexibility to re-sign Ayo Dosunmu and have available the $15M non tax midlevel and $5.5M biannual exception. Minnesota will also create a $33.3M trade exception.

item.title: Naz Reid will be the starting power forward for Wolves after Julius Randle trade
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Naz Reid is going to be the starting power forward for the Minnesota Timberwolves for the first time in his career. This move is a big statement of belief in Reid and Jaden McDaniels to take the next steps in their development.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu will sign five-year deal to remain in Minnesota
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Ayo Dosunmu intends to sign a five-year, $112 million contract to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves, with a player option in the fifth season, sources tell ESPN. Timberwolves made it a major priority to lock in Dosunmu after his tremendous postseason.

item.title: Dosunmu’s agents, Mike Lindeman and Jeff Schwartz of …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dosunmu’s agents, Mike Lindeman and Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management, worked with Timberwolves executives on Monday night to land the long-term commitment. Dosunmu and Anthony Edwards gained a chemistry after his arrival from Chicago in February.

item.title: Wolves not interested in Ja Morant
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves have not been in the Ja Morant mix. Decision was made to look elsewhere for backcourt help

item.title: Bulls are trading Mo Gueye to Minnesota, sources say, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bulls are trading Mo Gueye to Minnesota, sources say, to complete the three-team deal that sends Julius Randle and No. 28 in tomorrow’s NBA Draft to Brooklyn for No. 33, and reroutes Nic Claxton into Chicago’s cap space in the new league year. Gueye’s salary is non-guaranteed for 2026-27.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with Heat, Celtics and Timberwolves before trade
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The 3 teams Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with: Miami, Boston and Minnesota. The Heat land a foundational piece and now have the attention of top players searching for a new home.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per …
item.description: ‪Chris Hine‬: Ayo Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per year, higher than I think a lot of folks thought it might. One thing about the Connelly era -- a few more million than perceived isn't much for him to haggle over if he thinks you're a part of the core long term.

item.title: Jaden McDaniels on Julius Randle trade: 'Crazy'
item.description: Q What's up, Jaden? Did you see the Julius Randle news? Jaden McDaniels: What do you mean? Q. He just got traded. McDaniels: For real? Q. Brooklyn. McDaniels: Oh, that's crazy.

item.title: Timberwolves not targeting Ja Morant
item.description: The Wolves are not pursuing Memphis point guard Ja Morant, according to team sources, but will continue to canvas the league for other possibilities.

item.title: The NBA vultures are swirling around Ant …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: My question is what's Anthony Edwards thinking? Tim MacMahon: I was going to say we started off we just spent a half hour talking about the Giannis trade that we've been talking about for years, and I'm not declaring anything with certainty except for the fact that the NBA vultures are swirling around Ant in anticipation of him potentially becoming the next superstar who's available in the trade market.

item.title: Anthony Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves …
item.description: The Wolves could not afford to run it back again after making minimal changes to the group in the summer of 2025, especially after watching Towns win a championship in New York this season. Anthony Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves are banking on addition by subtraction, believing that removing Randle’s ISO-heavy offensive game and redistributing his 15.3 shots per game to McDaniels and Reid will open things up for Minnesota in a different way. McDaniels took a big step forward with

item.title: Naz Reid is now slated to be the primary starter at …
item.description: Naz Reid is now slated to be the primary starter at power forward for the first time in his career, a team source said. He has been the good soldier for the first seven seasons of his career, dutifully playing the role of super sub and winning Sixth Man of the Year in 2024. He has taken a backseat to Towns and Randle, but always quietly pined for the starting spot. Now he has it.

item.title: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. …
item.description: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. That’s 150 first-round picks. First question: did they make an impact for more than a month or two for the team that drafted them? The obvious choices are Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball (2020); Cade Cunningham (2021) and Stephon Castle (2024). Throw in guys like Onyeka Okongwu (2020) and Anthony Black (2023) who clearly are difference makers for the teams that drafted them or traded for them on draft night. How many of that 150 total made t

item.title: I will be incredibly surprised if the Wolves ever …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: I will be incredibly surprised if the Wolves ever utilize the $33 million trade exception that was created tonight. Those things are almost always much ado about nothing and it is clear that money was a factor tonight.

item.title: Blazers to hire Timberwolves lead assistant Micah Nori as head coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: The Portland Trail Blazers are hiring Minnesota Timberwolves lead assistant Micah Nori as the franchise's next head coach, sources tell ESPN. Nori -- widely regarded as one of the league's top assistants -- has been in the NBA coaching ranks since 2009 and now takes over in Portland on a multiyear deal.

item.title: Nori was lead assistant of the Minnesota Timberwolves …
item.description: Jason Quick: BREAKING: Micah Nori has agreed to become the head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers, sources tell @TheAthletic . Nori was lead assistant of the Minnesota Timberwolves for the past five years. Nori, 52, signed a one-year deal with team options for each of the next two seasons.

item.title: “I think he’s come late to the (head-coaching) party, …
item.description: “I think he’s come late to the (head-coaching) party, but that doesn’t mean he’s not more than ready and won’t be successful,” Minnesota coach Chris Finch told The Athletic. Added Timberwolves assistant Elston Turner: “A good pick. He’s worked for a lot of teams, been in a lot of positions, been with some big-name players. A good, good pick.”

item.title: “He is elite in what I call the small pieces of the …
item.description: In Minnesota, where the Timberwolves have won five playoff series over the past three years, Finch entrusted Nori with handling the details of the game. “He is elite in what I call the small pieces of the game,” Finch said. “Small not that they are unimportant, but small in that they are often overlooked. He’s elite with lineup combinations. Elite with rotations. Elite with special situations, whether it’s ATOs (after timeouts), end of game or just understanding how to maximize possessions. He’s

item.title: Yes, Ant was frustrated by how last season ended. But …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Yes, Ant was frustrated by how last season ended. But he has also already won a lot here and believes in his teammates and the org to keep upgrading. He's under contract for 3 more years. Orgs are always on the clock with star players, but Wolves have time here.

item.title: Julius Randle excited to join Brooklyn Nets
item.description: Julius Randle is excited to join the Nets and wants to be in Brooklyn, league sources told HoopsHype. After coming to New York amid a rebuilding situation, Randle evolved into a three-time All-Star and two-time All-NBA forward who helped the crosstown rival Knicks reach consecutive Eastern Conference Semifinals appearances before being dealt to Minnesota as part of the Karl-Anthony Towns trade. Randle is owed $33.33 million for the upcoming season and has a $35.80 million player option for the 2

item.title: Anthony Edwards still committed to Minnesota
item.description: Everyone wants to know what Anthony Edwards thinks. Multiple sources close to Edwards dismissed the chatter as just that. One said there was no truth to any of it. Another reiterated Edwards and his team love Minnesota, and they think being here, in this location with this franchise is the “ideal” situation.

item.title: Edwards wants to remain a part of a highly competitive …
item.description: Yes, Edwards wants to remain a part of a highly competitive team that is capable of winning playoff series and contending for championships. But there is a faith in the team and in President of Basketball Operations Tim Connelly to do what’s necessary to ensure that vision now and into the future.

item.title: People close to Anthony Edwards dismiss trade rumors
item.description: Multiple sources close to Edwards dismissed the chatter as just that. One said there was no truth to any of it. Another reiterated Edwards and his team love Minnesota, and they think being here, in this location with this franchise, is the “ideal” situation.

item.title: League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are …
item.description: A subplot I'm watching tonight: Will Trey Murphy III finally get traded out of New Orleans? There has been all kinds of chatter about the 26-year-old small forward being on the move of late, with a laundry list of teams interested. League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are currently vetting scenarios in which they'd trade their pick (No. 8 and No. 11, respectively) to land him. He's been tied to Brooklyn (pre-Julius Randle trade), the Clippers (No. 5 pick) and Boston (No. 27) as well.

item.title: Chris Finch on Terrence Shannon and Jaylen Clark: We got to figure out how to get these guys a more consistent role
item.description: ‪Ryan Eichten‬: Chris Finch on what he sees for Terrence Shannon and Jaylen Clark: "It's about creating pathways for those guys to be able to play. You might say like, isn't that the coach's job? But sometimes, you know, there's a gridlock. So we've gotta look at how we can either kind of change things around or get to a point where these guys can get a more consistent role." (2/2)

item.title: Winfield: Mikel Brown says he’s looking forward to …
item.description: Kristian Winfield: Mikel Brown says he’s looking forward to learning from Julius Randle and Michael Porter Jr. with Nets: “I think it’s great. You want to soak up as much knowledge as possible as a young player in this league.” Ends with “We’re gonna try to bring a chip.”

item.title: "Certainly through a modern lens. He's got a great …
item.description: Dane Moore: Chris Finch said he and Micah Nori "see the game the same way". Asked Finch how he would describe the way Nori sees the game. "Certainly through a modern lens. He's got a great kind of balance between the freedom of the game, how analytics feeds into that, allowing players to migrate to their strengths, keeping things pretty simple... I just love the simplicity with which he sees the game."

item.title: Knicks do not expect Sergio De Larrea to be on roster next season
item.description: Jake Fischer: Had heard De Larrea for both New York and Minnesota, before the Timberwolves traded No. 28, had appeal because of his draft-and-stash opportunity. Wolves obviously used that pick to get off Julius Randle's money. Knicks don't expect De Larrea to come to the NBA this season.

item.title: It's the No. 35 pick in tomorrow's second round, a …
item.description: Jake Fischer: It's the No. 35 pick in tomorrow's second round, a 2031 Sacramento second-round pick and a 2028 Minnesota second round pick, per source.

item.title: “I thought it was a shame that a lot of the story …
item.description: Nori’s former boss, Minnesota Timberwolves head coach Chris Finch, said Tuesday that he wished more attention had been paid to Nori’s accomplishment than to the terms of his contract. “I thought it was a shame that a lot of the story about Micah’s great opportunity was overshadowed by the nature of the deal that he signed,” Finch told reporters before the NBA Draft. “That’s a personal decision. That’s a business decision. That’s between him, the team and the agent.”

item.title: I know he wasn’t focused on certainly money, but he …
item.description: “I know this: There’s 30 of these jobs,” Finch said, “They’re hard to get. They’re incredibly rewarding in a lot of different ways. I know he wasn’t focused on certainly money, but he was first focused on opportunity. That’s what we preach to all of our guys here. You should chase opportunity, not money. “Then, I also know that you’ve got to bet on yourself in this world,” Finch said. “Especially when you’re an outsider. … We consider ourselves a little bit of outsiders. We weren’t raised in the

item.title: Brooklyn selects Joshua Jefferson at No. 28 via Minnesota trade
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Joshua Jefferson with the No. 28 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Brooklyn acquired this pick from the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team deal, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Chicago Bulls.

item.title: Connelly when asked what his conversations with …
item.description: Dane Moore: Tim Connelly when asked what his conversations with ownership have been like about potentially paying the luxury tax for a third season in a row. "It's about winning. If the moves can exponentially increase our chance to win at the highest level, then we're all in. We can't be irresponsible fiscally. We can't ignore the real realities and the increasing punishment as you get deeper and deeper into the tax. But we're here to win. We're to try to get over the hump."

item.title: Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony …
item.description: Lakers draft pick Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony Edwards: "Yeah, I ain't dealing with the flopping, Anthony Edwards"

item.title: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete …
item.description: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete a trade for Antetokounmpo is that they resisted the Bucks’ demands that Jaden McDaniels be included in the deal, a team source said. “I think the whole room is excited,” Connelly said. “I think we’re ready to continue to try to take the next step. But Ant’s just such a competitive guy. He wants to make sure we’re moving in the right direction and we keep getting better and better.”

item.title: One of those players, team sources who have been …
item.description: Connelly said on Tuesday night that the Timberwolves were trying to get back into the first round when a few players they liked started to fall, but they were unable to complete a deal. One of those players, team sources who have been granted anonymity to discuss nonpublic information said, was Alabama guard Labaron Philon, who went to Philadelphia at No. 22.

item.title: It doesn’t matter that, according to multiple people …
item.description: It doesn’t matter that Edwards is under contract for three more years. It doesn’t matter that he has made the most money a player his age could possibly make in the NBA. It doesn’t matter that, according to multiple people with knowledge of Edwards’ thinking, the Timberwolves star is not flinching at the challenge ahead and believes the team has a bright future ahead of it. It doesn’t matter that the Timberwolves have put a veteran team around him with good leaders that has won five playoff seri

item.title: Edwards is happy in Minnesota.
item.description: Edwards is happy in Minnesota. Despite his global reach as one of the most popular young players in the league, a signature pitchman for Adidas, Sprite and more, and his supporting role in Adam Sandler’s move “Hustle,” Edwards enjoys relative solitude here. He likes to go to work and then return to his home with his family and friends unbothered, and that is exactly what he is allowed to do.

item.title: As soon as the Timberwolves were eliminated by San …
item.description: It isn’t just media talking heads who love to play this card. Executives across the NBA are constantly combing the rosters to see where the next vulnerability will surface, where the next star player can be swiped. As soon as the Timberwolves were eliminated by San Antonio in the second round of the playoffs last month, front office personnel from other teams started to check in with me, wondering about Edwards’ patience with the Timberwolves’ direction. The game is the game.

item.title: "'It's far in the future, but would you want to be a …
item.description: Ayo Dosunmu: "'It's far in the future, but would you want to be a coach or work in a front office after playing?' I would definitely want to be a probably a GM."

item.title: Anthony Edwards the next star to watch potentially hit the trade block?
item.description: Windhorst: "If you say to me Giannis is now been traded, who would be the next NBA star that you would watch? The answer would be Anthony Edwards. Already, Anthony Edwards has watched Karl-Anthony Towns, a player he totally fit with on the court, be traded to the Knicks and win a championship. Last summer, they let Nickeill Alexander-Walker go, he goes and wins most improved player down in Atlanta."

item.title: Isaiah Evans heads to Minnesota at No. 33 via trade
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Isaiah Evans with the No. 33 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Evans is headed to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team trade, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Bulls.

item.title: Wolves select Trey Kaufman-Renn at No. 59
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves will select Trey Kaufman-Renn with the 59th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: Wolves, Raptors emerge as teams interested in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Marc Stein: The Timberwolves and Raptors have emerged as two of the teams with trade interest in Charlotte’s LaMelo Ball, league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me. More to come …

item.title: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Toronto Raptors and Minnesota Timberwolves, sources confirm. Toronto originally expressed trade interest in Ball during the summer of 2025 and renewed talks with the Charlotte Hornets over the past few days.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire to acquire Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball, league sources tell me. Hornets only listening to offers at the moment.

item.title: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to acquire LaMelo Ball. They'd want to do a 100 percent salary match so they can exceed the first apron, which can be done by including Donte DiVincenzo. Then it's about adding draft picks & young players to incentivize Charlotte.

item.title: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, keep an eye on the three-team trade that the Wolves agreed to with the Brooklyn Nets and Chicago Bulls becoming an expanded trade to include the Charlotte Hornets. That would allow Minnesota to use Julius Randle and the outgoing salary-match (he'd still end up Brooklyn). Would need other pieces involved too but could work for the teams involved.

item.title: Jaden McDaniels off limits in trade conversations
item.description: Minnesota has maintained that perimeter defensive ace Jaden McDaniels is off limits in trade conversations. Sources say that the Wolves also prefer to "do right" by injured guard Donte DiVincenzo and keep the Villanova product out of their offseason business after he sustained a devastating torn Achilles during Minnesota's first-round playoff victory over Denver. The Wolves, sources say, would also prefer not to part with 2024-25 Sixth Man of the Year Naz Reid, but it is difficult to see how the

item.title: "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most …
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have reportedly emerged as the strongest candidate to pursue the Charlotte Hornets star, with league sources continuing to connect Ball to a potential partnership with Anthony Edwards. Speaking about the situation, MacMahon explained that Minnesota is the team being discussed most frequently among league executives and insiders. "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most from people around the league is the Minnesota Timberwolves. And it's no secret," he said p

item.title: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on the Timberwolves potentially trading Rudy Gobert for LaMelo Ball: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go forward. They won the series against the Nuggets because of Gobert. There is a height and size race in the Western Conference right now. So I'm not sure what the pathway is. I guess it's possible that they could expand the Julius Randle trade to a multiple team trade to open this door, but I do think Minnesota feels like they have to look at this, and I think Charl

item.title: The Timberwolves were one of several teams to express …
item.description: After ESPN reported Wednesday night that the Charlotte Hornets were fielding offers for Ball, the Timberwolves were one of several teams to express interest in trading for the star point guard, team and league sources told The Athletic. The people spoke on the condition of anonymity because discussions were still in the very early stages.

item.title: The Randle trade is not expected to become official …
item.description: The Randle trade is not expected to become official for several weeks because both the Nets and Wolves have to work through other moves first to maximize their dealmaking abilities under the rules of the collective bargaining agreement. “We’ll always kind of invent a way to transact even if, at times, on paper, it doesn’t look as though we can,” Lloyd said. “Something will happen. I don’t know how. We’ll have mechanisms to utilize here in the next couple weeks, too.”

item.title: Timberwolves trade big haul, including Naz Reid, to Charlotte for star point guard LaMelo Ball
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Charlotte Hornets are trading star guard LaMelo Ball and Josh Green to the Minnesota Timberwolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick, three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033), sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a star-studded new backcourt in Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball -- the Nos. 1 and 3 picks in 2020. Charlotte didn't shop Ball in the market, but listened and received multiple rich offers before filling a significant need in the frontcourt with Reid. Timberwolves co-owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for the third straight year -- and the franchise makes a big move to fill the point guard spot.

item.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball in exchange for for Naz Reid, a first round pick, three first round pick swaps and three second round picks.

item.title: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to …
item.description: Marc Stein: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to Minnesota, league sources say, after @TheSteinLine revealed Minnesota’s interest last night. The Hornets have successfully pried Naz Reid away from the Wolves and will now try to retain Pistons target Coby White in free agency.

item.title: The easiest path could be to fold this deal into the …
item.description: Keith Smith: Multiple ways the LaMelo Ball trade could be completed, but unless other parts of this emerge later, the easiest path could be to fold this deal into the previously agreed to trade with the Nets, Bulls and Timberwolves. That would solve salary-matching issues for the Wolves.

item.title: Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Timebrwolves will need to fold the LaMelo Ball trade with Nets/Bulls trade since their post-trade payroll is above the first apron. Julius Randle & Naz Reid for LaMelo Ball & Josh Green is a 100 percent salary match. They're now hard capped to second apron for aggregation.

item.title: Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from Minnesota to land Naz Reid plus an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 as well as three first-round pick swaps and three second-round picks. It is expected this trade will be folded into the three-team Julius Randle deal.

item.title: Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid. Josh Green for Naz Reid is a clean salary match, allowing them to create a $40.8 million trade exception for Ball. They also have 12 future first-round picks through 2033.

item.title: Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a …
item.description: ‪‪Chris Hine‬: LaMelo Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a great backcourt mate with Anthony Edwards and think he can be a winning player, despite public perception. The question was if he’d ever become available. He did, and here we are.

item.title: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov‬: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected 1st round pick, swaps in 2028, 2029, and 2030, and 3 second round picks, a league source confirmed. Shams was first on it. A huge swing for both teams.

item.title: “He’s (Ant) is tired of being double teamed. They get …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Brian Windhorst on LaMelo the Minnesota: “He’s (Ant) is tired of being double teamed. They get him a dynamic scorer in the backcourt. You throw a double at Ant Edwards with Lamelo Ball on the court, you are playing with fire. Lamelo will drop 10 3s on you in a blink of an eye”

item.title: Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for …
item.description: ‪Shams Charania Tweets‬: Timberwolves co-owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for the third straight year -- and the franchise makes a big move to fill the point guard spot.

item.title: $75 million in room below the luxury tax line. Enough …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Charlotte Hornets salary cap situation after trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid and picks. $75 million in room below the luxury tax line. Enough room to re-sign Coby White, use the entirety of their $40.8 million trade exception, and $15 million mid-level.

item.title: Timberwolves cap situation after trading for LaMelo …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Minnesota Timberwolves cap situation after trading for LaMelo Ball. He has a small trade bonus that increases his salary to the $41.25 million max. They have $11 million under second apron hard cap to fill 4 roster spots. They will no longer have a $33 million trade exception.

item.title: Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Minnesota Timberwolves project to be $4.7M over the luxury tax after adding LaMelo Ball and Josh Green. Minnesota will be $3.3M under the first apron and could trigger a first-apron hard cap (taking back more salary than they send out or using the NTMLE as a TPE). 1/2

item.title: @boogiecousins says trading for LaMelo Ball is a great …
item.description: Run It Back: . @boogiecousins says trading for LaMelo Ball is a great move for the Minnesota Timberwolves 🔥 "They've been searching for a point guard to play with Anthony Edwards... could be really special, something the league hasn't seen." @MichelleDBeadle | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: Wolves have been trying to get LaMelo Ball for two …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves have been trying to get LaMelo Ball for two years. They paid a price with Naz, but there is elation throughout the building about pairing Melo and Anthony Edwards.

item.title: “He’s going to make everybody better. That was the …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: “He’s going to make everybody better. That was the thing that was missing." -- Wolves source on the addition of LaMelo Ball.

item.title: "This is a plus for LaMelo and the Minnesota …
item.description: Run It Back: Boogie Cousins thinks LaMelo Ball's immaturity issues will go away playing with Anthony Edwards. "This is a plus for LaMelo and the Minnesota Timberwolves." @MichelleDBeadle | @boogiecousins | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: Without draft picks, they might have to include …
item.description: Sam Quinn: If Minnesota is keeping Donte DiVincenzo, they somehow have to turn Josh Green's $14.7 million salary into a playable power forward. Without draft picks, they might have to include Terrence Shannon to do so. But that's the next order of business here.

item.title: Timberwolves had discussions with Celtics on potential Jaylen Brown trade before LaMelo Ball deal
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves did have discussions with the Celtics on Jaylen Brown as well, sources told The Athletic. But they saw LaMelo Ball as fitting more of what they needed, for likely less compensation going out.

item.title: "LaMelo Ball is very excited to go play with Anthony …
item.description: First Take: "LaMelo Ball is very excited to go play with Anthony Edwards to try to win championships. He knows the task at hand." @ShamsCharania breaks down the LaMelo Ball trade to Minnesota ✍️

item.title: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to …
item.description: Jazz owner Ryan Smith: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to start winning. I think that's that's that's kind of where we're at. When we took over, I took over what was the number one team in the regular season and we got, I mean, it was interesting. My first day was like post-COVID. We were kind of into COVID and I had like two weeks to get a deal done with Rudy Gobert and Donovan Mitchell. It was really complicated because normally you have a long time to do this, but that tea

item.title: Timebrwolves not trading Donte DiVincenzo
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves do still have other moves in the works. They need a power forward and they need more shooting. I am told that they are not trading Donte DiVincenzo. The work continues.

item.title: "The best part of my job, my favorite thing that I do, …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Joe Cronin: "The best part of my job, my favorite thing that I do, is I have a front-row seat to watch people live their dreams. It's such a cool and unique thing to watch people do what they love and go on their paths. To be next to Micah through this, couldn't be more excited." Micah Nori starts out by thanking the Timberwolves organization. "Without them and the success we've had the last five years, I'm not here."

item.title: A car belonging to the Minnesota Timberwolves' Jaden …
item.description: A car belonging to the Minnesota Timberwolves' Jaden McDaniels was stolen at the 25-year-old's Los Angeles home, the LAPD told NBC4 Investigates. McDaniels walked outside his residence Wednesday morning to find his 2022 Dodge Charger missing from his parking space. McDaniels, whose team was at the center of NBA trade talk Thursday morning, visited a LAPD station, where officers took at stolen car report. Law enforcement sources told NBC4 Investigates they do not believe the former Washington Hus

item.title: (Jaden McDaniels, who rival teams have sternly been …
item.description: (Jaden McDaniels, who rival teams have sternly been sent in the opposite direction when inquiring, sources say, is considered part of Minnesota's new big three.)

item.title: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as …
item.description: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as potentially an option there (for Minnesota). Now, I think that's going to depend on what his free agent market place ends up being."

item.title: "I’ve interviewed for so many jobs, I think it was a …
item.description: For Nori, this is the realization of a dream almost three decades in the making as a lifer assistant coach with five different organizations across 28 years in the NBA, most recently with the Minnesota Timberwolves for the last five seasons. He's interviewed for numerous head coaching jobs in that time, including the Lakers and Cavaliers openings in 2024 and the Knicks job that went to Mike Brown last summer, perpetually finishing second or third in those searches. "I’ve interviewed for so many

item.title: I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "They were in on Jaylen Brown as well. It was made clear by the Celtics that he was going to be available and the Wolves were trying to have conversations about what it would take to get Jaylen Brown. I think that they looked at those two, Jaylen Brown and LaMelo Ball, and Brown, just because he's won a championship, he's a little older, he was top-six MVP this year, I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown than what the Hornets wanted for Ball."

item.title: LaVar Ball to Minnesota: 'You got to get the other two Ball boys'
item.description: LaVar Ball: "Minnesota Timberwolves, y'all did the coldest move in the game. You went and got that Melo. Yes, but here is the thing, let me give you the recipe. You got to get the other two Ball boys. You get the other two Ball boys, it will be the most watched team ever in the NBA. Trust me when I tell you. You don't think I'm just talking? No, I'm not."

item.title: Timberwolves explored James Harden, Ja Morant as point guard options
item.description: At the same time, teams like Minnesota and Toronto — franchises that had been extremely aggressive in pursuing upgrades — found themselves with an opportunity to improve their fortunes. The Wolves, after sending Julius Randle to Brooklyn, were left with a $33 million trade exception and a cap book $66 million leaner, while continuing to signal intentions to add a quality point guard, sources say (a pursuit that at one time included Cleveland's James Harden and Memphis' Ja Morant). The Raptors, w

item.title: The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point …
item.description: Ball, 24, is under contract for the next three seasons and is set to make $40.8 million next year as a running mate for Anthony Edwards in Minnesota. The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point guard and league sources confirmed to MassLive that the Celtics did not pursue a deal for the former Hornets guard.

item.title: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a simple question, what do you think of the LaMelo deal? And one replied one sentence, Tim is a riverboat gambler. And this is big-time, high-stakes gambling that he's doing right here. And you understand the swing that he's taking, you understand the hole in the roster it's creating. Chris Finch is also one of the most creative coaches in the league and he's going to earn his money figuring out how to make this work."

item.title: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch have long believed that LaMelo Ball is a perfect fit for Anthony Edwards in that backcourt. And I know that over the last several years, they have checked in on LaMelo and Charlotte a couple of times. Each time they were turned away, and so something has happened in the last couple of days or weeks to get the Charlotte Hornets to a point where they were comfortable parting with LaMelo Ball."

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a 5% to 10% stake in the NBA franchise to private equity firm Blue Owl. The deal values the NBA team and Cleveland WNBA team set to start play in 2028 at a combined $5.5 billion, according to someone familiar with the agreement. Blue Owl’s sports strategy fund buys limited partner stakes in NBA franchises, a fund originally named Dyal HomeCourt. The fund already has stakes in the Atlanta Hawks, Charlotte Hornets, Minnesota Timberwolves and Sacra

item.title: However, the Wolves can no longer function as a …
item.description: This trade, which can’t be official until July 6 since the Bulls required cap space to acquire Nic Claxton, will be expanded to include the Wolves’ acquisition of Ball for several reasons. The first is that Reid’s $23.3 million salary isn’t enough to match Ball’s $41.25 million individually. Randle’s $33.3 million salary is ($9,098,000 trade exception). Then they could simply trade Reid for Green and create a trade exception worth the difference in their salaries. However, the Wolves can no long

item.title: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin …
item.description: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin in the frontline. They will have a difficult time finding an upgrade with no draft picks left to trade. They might need to part with one of Terrence Shannon Jr. or Joan Beringer to get a team interested. Otherwise, they’re looking at starting a lineup featuring LaMelo Ball, Ayo Dosunmu, Anthony Edwards, Jaden McDaniels, and Rudy Gobert. The Wolves would have limited options for upgrading the frontcourt. They will no longer create a $33 million

item.title: Celtics wanted Rudy Gobert, Naz Reid, first-round pick and pick swaps from Timberwolves for Jaylen Brown
item.description: The Timberwolves explored multiple avenues to add another star next to Edwards in the days leading up to landing Ball. There were discussions with the Chicago Bulls about Josh Giddey. They had talks with the Boston Celtics about Jaylen Brown, a team source said, but the Celtics were looking for a package including Gobert, Reid and similar pick compensation that went to Charlotte for Ball, team sources said. That was just too much for the Wolves to pay for a soon-to-be 30-year-old forward who, wh

item.title: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that …
item.description: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that the Hornets were seriously entertaining offers on Ball, and the Timberwolves wasted no time. The final offer for Ball was prepared and sent to owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez for review and approval early Thursday morning, according to multiple team sources that spoke on the condition of anonymity because the trade has not been officially announced.

item.title: And immediately gave the green light, team sources …
item.description: Lore and Rodriguez looked at the offer — former Sixth Man of the Year and Timberwolves cult hero Naz Reid, an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 and three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033) to Charlotte for Ball and swingman Josh Green — and immediately gave the green light, team sources said.

item.title: “We needed a guy like LaMelo to make everybody …
item.description: Ball averaged 20.1 points, 7.1 assists and 4.8 rebounds last season. There are questions about his decision-making and the seriousness with which he takes the game, but the Wolves love his size, shot creation and playmaking. Maybe even more importantly, the Hornets were 11.6 points per 100 possessions better on offense with Ball on the floor last season, per Cleaning The Glass. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte’s terrific rookie, made 46 percent of his 3-pointers when Ball was on the court. That number di

item.title: Anthony Edwards has long admired LaMelo Ball's game
item.description: Ball’s ability to initiate offense, shoot 3-pointers at a high volume and see over the top of the defense with his 6-foot-7 frame to find open shooters is exactly what the Wolves were missing. Ball, who was the No. 3 pick in the same 2020 draft class that saw Edwards chosen No. 1, is also just 24. He fits with the timeline of a young core that is tip-toeing into its prime, and he arrives with the tacit approval of Edwards, who has long admired Ball’s game, sources said.

item.title: “He makes Ant better, Jaden (McDaniels) better, Rudy …
item.description: “He makes Ant better, Jaden (McDaniels) better, Rudy and Joan (Beringer) better,” one team source said. “How many other players in the league would make your three best players better without getting in their way?”

item.title: Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at …
item.description: Just a few days earlier, Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at power forward for the first time in his career when the Timberwolves traded Randle to Brooklyn. But the Hornets insisted on Reid being in the trade, sources said.

item.title: They do not have a traditional power forward on the …
item.description: They do not have a traditional power forward on the roster, and league sources say the Wolves are looking at several options for help at the four. They are also in the market to add more shooting around Ball and Edwards. The work continues, but the magnitude of the Ball trade means that the major deals are done now.

item.title: "It was almost unanimously that the Charlotte Hornets …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "It was almost unanimously that the Charlotte Hornets were the biggest winner. There is a lot of respect for Tim Connelly. People call him a riverboat gambler. I had some people say I didn't like the Rudy Gobert deal, and that worked out great for the Timberwolves. So, with those caveats, the almost consensus opinion of the people I talked to around the league was LaMelo Ball had next to no value a year ago. Jeff Peterson, the Charlotte Hornets general manager, cleaned him up and a

item.title: Krawczynski:Jaylen Clark, a restricted free agent, …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski:Jaylen Clark, a restricted free agent, will be returning to the Timberwolves, as most expected. Defensive stalwart is also a good cutter, which should benefit him when he shares the floor with LaMelo Ball.

item.title: Jaylen Clark re-signing with the Timberwolves on a …
item.description: Chris Hine: Jaylen Clark re-signing with the Timberwolves on a 3-year deal worth $10 million, per a source.

item.title: A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors …
item.description: That doesn’t mean that they weren’t or won’t be in the mix for other players. A couple league sources confirmed that the Raptors were interested in getting involved in talks with the Charlotte Hornets for mercurial point guard LaMelo Ball, a player Webster has always liked, which is one of the reasons he was intrigued by the Hornets' top job before current Charlotte GM Jeff Peterson was hired in 2024.

item.title: Gueye is set to be traded to the Minnesota …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Chicago Bulls have picked up their $2.4M team option for Mouhamadou Gueye, a league source told @spotrac. Gueye's contract is now fully non-guaranteed until January 7. Gueye is set to be traded to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of the Bulls-Wolves-Nets-Hornets trade.

item.title: LaMelo Ball ranked second in the NBA (behind Jokic) in …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: Interesting wrinkle of the LaMelo trade: According to an analysis I'm running for a future article, LaMelo Ball ranked second in the NBA (behind Jokic) in raising his teammates TS% Expect everyone on the Hornets to shoot a little worse next season, and the Wolves to shoot better

item.title: Timberwolves have tendered a qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Minnesota Timberwolves have tendered a qualifying offer to Jaylen Clark and a two-way qualifying offer to Enrique Freeman, a league source told @spotrac. Clark and Freeman will now be restricted free agents this offseason. Clark has reportedly agreed to a new deal already.

item.title: Spurs, Magic and Sixers interested in John Collins
item.description: League sources say John Collins certainly has fans in Minnesota, but it appears unlikely that the Timberwolves will have sufficient spending power to pursue the scoring forward, who spent last season with the Clippers. Collins is also expected to draw interest from Orlando, San Antonio and Philadelphia once free agency begins. The 76ers and Western Conference champion Spurs continue to get linked in free agency projections to the top forwards in the marketplace.

item.title: I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim …
item.description: Barry Jackson: With regard to the Heat report by the very hard-working @TheSteinLine and @JakeLFischer , I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim Hardaway Jr. when free agency begins Tuesday night, per a source. This would be a very sensible marriage if it happens... With regard to Mike Conley, he has received some consideration internally. But there are some internally who would prefer going a younger route.

item.title: Nuggets looking for another ballhandler
item.description: The Nuggets are not allowed to discuss the extension with Jokić until July 6 and, per team sources, don’t yet have clarity about how he will handle it. In the interim, front-office co-heads Jon Wallace and Ben Tenzer are focused on improving a team that fell to the Minnesota Timberwolves in the first round in late April. More specifically, league sources say, the Nuggets are on the lookout for another creative ballhandler.

item.title: "I confirmed tonight that Dave Joerger has interviewed …
item.description: Marc Stein: "I confirmed tonight that Dave Joerger has interviewed for the (Nuggets assistant) post, but here's the thing, Dave Joerger is also being pursued very heavily by Paris Basketball, which is the team that is run by former Timberwolves front office lead decision maker David Kahn."

item.title: Sam Amick: Celtics are asking for the sun and the moon in Jaylen Brown trade talks
item.description: Sam Amick on Jaylen Brown: "This one is, if I had to handicap it, it just feels like somebody has got to put enough on the table to get this thing done, but Boston is asking for the sun and the moon. I keep looking at Portland and because people keep bringing Portland up when I talk to them, they wanted to go big on Giannis Antetokounmpo and that did not work. Even without a commitment, they were willing to try to do a Giannis trade. And when you see that kind of boldness, then you kind of go, a

item.title: Timberwolves are declining the team option on Julian …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Timberwolves are declining the team option on Julian Phillips, a source told @TheAthletic.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves will decline their $2.41 …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Minnesota Timberwolves will decline their $2.41 million team option on Julian Phillips and will not extend him a qualifying offer, thus making him an unrestricted free agent, league sources told @hoopshype.

item.title: The Timberwolves were surging during the 2023-24 …
item.description: JC Stroebel and Jesse George started this whole thing on a whim. The Timberwolves were surging during the 2023-24 season, and Stroebel just threw out a post on social media offering to tattoo “Naz Reid.” on any who wanted one for $20. He had no idea what was coming. Wolves fans responded in overwhelming fashion. Stroebel and George estimate they’ve done between, at least, 1,200 to 1,500 “Naz Reid.” tattoos. What started as a lark with the hope of landing tickets to a playoff game became a full-b

item.title: Timberwolves have interest in LeBron James?
item.description: Cleveland and Miami are often cited as realistic landing spots as well, and the Minnesota Timberwolves are known to have interest if he felt like shocking the basketball world with that sort of move.

item.title: Bones Hyland returning to Minnesota
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Bones Hyland has agreed to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves next season, sources told @TheAthletic. Hyland established himself as a rotation player, teaming with Ayo Dosunmu in the "Twin Turbos" backcourt.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard was not interested in Minnesota and Detroit when they attempted to trade for him
item.description: APHoops: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Detroit Pistons were amongst the multiple teams attempting to trade for Kawhi Leonard before they were rebuffed by Leonard’s camp, per @ShamsCharania.

item.title: "I was supposed to coach Summer League last year in …
item.description: Williams said he's "pretty excited" about serving as a Summer League coach for the first time in his career. "It seemed like every time I had the opportunity to be a head coach for Summer League I either took another job or unfortunately early in my career (staffs he was a part of) got fired in a couple of situations," he said. "I was supposed to coach Summer League last year in Minnesota, but I was able to come here, so I'm definitely excited about this chance."

item.title: Minnesota governor also recruiting LeBron James
item.description: If Minnesota is indeed interested, the team is already getting a bump in recruitment from one notable resident. Minnesota Governor Tim Walz responded to a tweet of the report of James' Lakers departure with a simple message for The King. It's a screenshot of an article from the Minnesota Star Tribune with a headline that says "Minnesota ranked best in country for quality of life." It seems like a long-shot pitch, but hey, that might just appeal to a 41-year-old nearing retirement.

item.title: Timberwolves targeting LeBron James
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have precious little money to spend and a freezing cold winter waiting ahead, which makes luring LeBron James in free agency one of the longest of long shots. With a player as talented and cerebral as James is, and a spot in their starting lineup that fits him perfectly, the Wolves had to at least let him know they would love to have him in Minnesota. And that’s exactly what they are doing. After James informed the Los Angeles Lakers he would not be returning and would

item.title: The Wolves do not believe they’re anywhere near the …
item.description: The Wolves do not believe they’re anywhere near the front of the line for James’ services, the team and league sources said, especially considering their lack of spending power. After agreeing to terms with Bones Hyland on a one-year, league minimum deal Tuesday, the Timberwolves have two roster spots to fill and just a hair above the league minimum for one of those spots. They could try to trade Josh Green, who was acquired in the Ball trade and has an expiring $14.7 million contract to try to

item.title: Celtics pick up veteran point guard Mike Conley Jr.
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Mike Conley Jr. has agreed to a one-year deal to sign with the Boston Celtics, sources tell ESPN. Conley becomes only the 14th player in NBA history to reach 20 seasons.

item.title: Mike Conley made the decision to head to Boston in the …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Mike Conley made the decision to head to Boston in the last day or so, sources told @TheAthletic . An essential member of the Timberwolves, on and off the court, for the last 3 1/2 years.

item.title: We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since …
item.description: We had a meeting at the first possible moment — 9 p.m. in L.A. — with little-used Indiana Pacers forward Solomon Hill, and realized our hopes of getting him for “just” four years and $40 million were unrealistic given the multiple rivals lining up to beat our offer (New Orleans eventually got him for four years and $52 million). We tried to get Eric Gordon to take $40 million, since he’d known our star guard, Mike Conley, since childhood; we didn’t know he had more than $50 million waiting for h

item.title: Thank you to the @Timberwolves organization, team, and …
item.description: Mike Conley: An incredible run! Thank you to the @Timberwolves organization, team, and fans for embracing me and my family, and making Minneapolis feel like home.

item.title: I had the good fortune to cover some real gems in my …
item.description: I had the good fortune to cover some real gems in my 22 years on the Timberwolves beat. Mike Conley is right near the top. I learned something about the game from him with every conversation. He was as gracious with his time as anyone. I understood the team better than ever thanks to him.

item.title: No team in the league currently has fewer draft assets …
item.description: No team in the league currently has fewer draft assets than the Lakers, who don’t control their own first-round pick until 2032 and their own second-round selection until 2033. The only other team I can remember emptying its asset cache in this way recently is the Minnesota Timberwolves. To say this deal must work is an understatement. It’s really hard to look at this as anything other than a desperation move for a much-maligned Lakers front office led by Rob Pelinka, who has consistently made t

item.title: "I think Melo went to a better situation. They think …
item.description: Lonzo Ball: "I think Melo went to a better situation. They think they are better, cool, whatever, that is cool, and then I think Melo went to a better spot."

item.title: Lonzo Ball defends LaMelo Ball's trade to the …
item.description: Lonzo Ball defends LaMelo Ball's trade to the Timberwolves: "I think this is a great fit for him. People are questioning the fit, but I think this is a great fit for him."

item.title: "You have the two best players under 25 on the same …
item.description: Lonzo Ball: "You have the two best players under 25 on the same team."

item.title: Timberwolves to add shooting big man?
item.description: Following the blockbuster LaMelo Ball trade, NBA executives around the league believe the Minnesota Timberwolves will now look to add a big man who can stretch the floor, with the working assumption that the organization could look to flip the expiring $14.68 million contract of Josh Green or look to dip into their taxpayer mid-level exception with $6.06 million. Minnesota also remains very bullish on 19-year-old center Joan Beringer, who’s expected to have a bigger role this upcoming season.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves are signing Enrique Freeman to …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Minnesota Timberwolves are signing Enrique Freeman to a two-way deal, league sources told @hoopshype. Freeman returns to Minnesota for a second season.

item.title: "Who know what they’re about to get into off the …

Even going to Lithuania, he was just pulled out of high school and the same exact reputation, all those qualities that you named just now, those were the same exact things that the baggage that he had. It is crazy to say that baggage of a 15-year-old, but he has been carrying that same rep for so many years. It was so surprised being in Lithuania, showing up to the gym every single day was a small town, you could just walk in. I was ther

item.title: Wolves, Warriors interested in Rui Hachimura?
item.description: As the market has developed, sources say Hachimura's representation has now been in contact with Minnesota and Golden State as well. The Timberwolves have a well-chronicled need for a power forward after committing to trade away Randle and Naz Reid so they can pair LaMelo Ball with Anthony Edwards and Hachimura is said to stand as their current top choice at the position.

item.title: "One thing that's actually never discussed with LaMelo …
item.description: Mirin Fader: "One thing that's actually never discussed with LaMelo is how much the locker rooms love him. They love him. Like, every place I've seen him, Chino Hills, Lithuania, Australia, Charlotte, which I went to a practice, I went to a shootaround when I was doing a Kon Knueppel feature and you can see all the teammates gravitate towards him because the same things that people are dissing, his humor, his fun, his, you know, charisma are what they are attracted to."

item.title: Tim Connelly and Rich Paul have had a strong …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski on LeBron James: Tim Connelly and Rich Paul have had a strong relationship for a long time. Also notable that Paul mentions Wolves ownership group. And “We got Jaden McDaniels.” Whatever happens, Wolves are at least in the mix. Would’ve been unfathomable just a few years ago.

item.title: Timberwolves bring Trey Lyles back to the NBA
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Trey Lyles have agreed to a 1-year deal. Lyles returns to the NBA after playing for Real Madrid. He’s averaged 7.6 points and 4.3 rebounds with the Jazz, Nuggets, Spurs, Pistons and Kings.

item.title: Timberwolves still hoping for a deal with LeBron James
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Minnesota Timberwolves continue to hope LeBron James will choose them in free agency, sources say. They'll keep a spot open. They view Trey Lyles as a floor-spacing big who shot 43% on 3-pointers for Real Madrid. Octagon's Alex Saratsis did Lyles' deal

item.description: Jon Krawczinsky: The Minnesota Timberwolves have agreed to terms on a one-year deal with Trey Lyles, sources told The Athletic. This does NOT rule out LeBron James, I’m told

item.title: Wolves have full belief they can land LeBron James
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have ramped up their push for LeBron James, with full belief that they are very much in the hunt to land one of the greatest players in NBA history, league sources told The Athletic. The sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations, said the Timberwolves have been in contact with James’ representatives with a bold pitch: team up with Anthony Edwards, LaMelo Ball, Jaden McDaniels and Rudy Gobert to go after the kind of championship that

item.description: LeBron James' odds of playing for the Minnesota Timberwolves in 2026-27 have climbed from 2.6% to 7.0% over the past 24 hours on Polymarket, a 4.4-point move.

item.description: The Sixers, Nuggets and Timberwolves, league sources say, all still believe that they are in this race to some degree and getting legitimate consideration from James and his camp alongside the Cavaliers, Warriors and Heat.

item.title: Many teams still believe they have a chance at LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Sixers, the Timberwolves, the Heat, your Nuggets, based on everything I have been told all weekend, they all still believe they are in with the chance. I think the Warriors were portrayed as an early favorite, not the early favorite, an early favorite, because they were the team that we knew was determined to go after LeBron if he parted with the Lakers. We had known that for weeks leading up to LeBron and Rich Paul actually informing the Lakers, yes, we are definitely moving on

It was absolutely incredible and unique, that entire message and the entire podcast, just to be so open about it. I think a lot of teams really are in the mix. I just think that there are viable cases to be made for Cleveland, Philadelphia, Miami, Golden State, and Dallas and the Timberwolves. I think those all those teams really are in play and I just feel like Ric

item.title: Lonzo Ball: "Obviously, I think we would be fine. It …
item.description: LaVar Ball: "How you think y'all would do all on the same team as grown men?" Lonzo Ball: "Obviously, I think we would be fine. It would be a good team, but I think that ship sailed."

item.title: “It’s a dream, man. It’s a dream,” a smiling Ball told …
item.description: Appearing on his father LaVar Ball's podcast alongside his older brothers, Lonzo and LiAngelo, the new Minnesota star expressed his excitement when he was asked what it feels like to be with the Timberwolves now. “It’s a dream, man. It’s a dream,” a smiling Ball told everyone while holding his newborn son, LaOne.

item.title: @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying …
item.description: Marc Stein: League sources tell @TheSteinLine that Rui Hachimura prioritized staying in Los Angeles and chose the Clippers over interest from Brooklyn and Dallas as well as interest we previously reported from Golden State/Minnesota.

item.description: Sam Amick: Story on Rui Hachimura signing with the Clippers, at @TheAthletic , with @DanWoikeSports and @LawMurrayTheNU Source says Rui, who wanted to stay in LA, prioritized Clippers over interest from Brooklyn, San Antonio, Golden State, Minnesota, and Portland.

Kevin Garnett did the same thing. I saw this clip of him walking into the [Timberwolves'] arena and [the fans] gave him a standing ovation and people to this day love him, and some people don't always feel—" (h/t @BucksLead)

item.title: Donovan Mitchell would embrace Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James
item.description: Donovan Mitchell also would embrace the Cavaliers reuniting with LeBron James if James makes the decision to sign with Cleveland, according to sources. The Cavaliers are among several leading suitors for James, joining Miami, Golden State, Philadelphia and Minnesota.

Maybe they change their tune on the Donte DiVincenzo expiring contract. Now, I would tell you right now, there's interest in signing DiVincenzo to an extension having Dante here the next few years. But it's not like they're married to that idea."

He is going to go through all of the options, talk things over with Rich Paul and his camp, and just really try to get a handle on what the best situation is for him. An

item.title: "I think the longer that this drags out, the better …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "I think the longer that this drags out, the better the Timberwolves' chances become. Because if you think about what LeBron has to process and has to consider, it is fair to say of all of the teams left, Minnesota is probably the most surprising one. Cold winters, no history of winning, a lot of things that go into it that just make you think, why would the Timberwolves be in the hunt? Now, they have a great case to make, if you ask me, with basketball fit ... I think that they

item.title: Miles Bridges, Luke Kennard deals expected to be completed early next week
item.description: John Gambadoro: The Miles Bridges trade and Luke Kennard signing are expected to be completed early next week. The initial trade with Charlotte, Minnesota and Brooklyn should be done this weekend. Phoenix can’t complete their transaction until that trade is completed. There is no risk of it falling apart, just timing.

item.title: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s …
item.description: However small the risk might be that Leonard’s contract would be voided, that was reason enough for the Raptors to play it safe by cooperating with the league’s preference to close the investigation before finalizing the trade. The NBA has only voided the contract of one player, Joe Smith, and that was after the Minnesota Timberwolves signed him to three consecutive below-market deals with the promise to sign him to a longer, richer deal in the future. In that case, the league voided Smith’s con
