item.title: No update. Wolves still haven’t been told no. Nothing …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski on LeBron James' free agency: No update. Wolves still haven’t been told no. Nothing has changed since I last wrote about it. They believe they have a chance. We’ll see

item.title: Micah Nori on Chris Finch: 'There's no paranoia with him'
item.description: "How much of his style helped you prepare for what you are about to embark upon?" Micah Nori: "The one thing that coach Finch is, he is very very good at what he does. Number one, there's no paranoia with him and the other thing is he will let you coach and gives you a voice. When those things happen, I was fortunate to be with him for a long time and work with him. Philosophy-wise and the way we think about basketball is very very similar."

item.title: Calling it "probably the most difficult" decision he …
item.description: Calling it "probably the most difficult" decision he has had to make, Jeff Peterson said the LaMelo Ball trade was done with the goal of building sustained success for the Charlotte Hornets to contend for a title down the road. The Hornets' president of basketball operations met with the media Saturday to explain the surprising decision to trade one of the franchise's most popular players after the trade became official. The Hornets sent Ball and Josh Green to Minnesota for Naz Reid, a 2033 unpr

item.title: "These decisions are challenging at times, but when …
item.description: "These decisions are challenging at times, but when you look at the totality of the season and everything of where we were, it's important to take an honest look in the mirror of where you guys are as a team," Peterson said at the news conference at the Las Vegas summer league. "And that's what I had to do at the end of the season. And it just felt like the goal is never to compete for a play-in spot. The goal isn't to get to the play-in or even the playoffs for one year. "I've said it plenty of

item.title: "I'll say it every day: LaMelo was tremendous in our …
item.description: "It's my job to not just look at half of last season or two-thirds of last season," Peterson said when asked what he saw in the second-half surge that led him to consider trading Ball. "I've been here for a little bit over two years now. So looking at the totality of the picture and also looking at trends along the league. Just making an objective decision in terms of just the timing and being honest with myself of where we really are as it compares to the rest of the league. "I'll say it every

item.title: "I do empathize with the fans and I understand just …
item.description: Still, Ball was the most popular player on the roster because of his exciting and unpredictable playing style. Peterson said he understands why some fans are unhappy with the decision to trade the 24-year-old former All-Star and Rookie of the Year. "I do empathize with the fans and I understand just where they're coming from," Peterson said when asked for his message to the fanbase. "If I'm honest, I love LaMelo. He's a tremendous human being. Of course, he's an enormous talent. He and I have a

item.title: Timberwolves sign Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Minnesota Timberwolves signed Isaiah Evans to a four-year, $9.3 million deal, which includes three fully guaranteed years and a fourth-year team option, league sources told @hoopshype. Evans was the 33rd pick of the 2026 NBA Draft and was an All-ACC Team selection at Duke.

item.title: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition …
item.description: Besiktas Istanbul have announced another addition ahead of their long-awaited return to the EuroLeague next season. The club officially confirmed the signing of former NBA guard Jaylen Nowell. The 27-year-old has appeared in more than 200 NBA games, representing the Minnesota Timberwolves, Memphis Grizzlies, Detroit Pistons, and New Orleans Pelicans, among other teams.

item.title: The Timberwolves will formally introduce LaMelo Ball …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Timberwolves will formally introduce LaMelo Ball and Josh Green on Tuesday

item.title: "I think Bron in Minnesota with Ant Man would be must …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: DeMarcus Cousins on where he wants to see LeBron James end up: "I think Bron in Minnesota with Ant Man would be must watch TV. They added LaMelo Ball who's also another exciting player. You put those three characters together I think we in for some good ball and possibly a championship run"

item.title: 10-year NBA veteran TJ Warren has signed a one-year …
item.description: 10-year NBA veteran TJ Warren has signed a one-year deal with EuroLeague team Paris Basketball, the French club offcially announced. On July 1, BasketNews' Donatas Urbonas reported that Warren was drawing significant interest from Europe as he explored a move overseas. Now, the 32-year-old wing is set for his first overseas season and contract. The 203-centimeter forward appeared in 385 NBA games across 10 seasons with the Phoenix Suns, Indiana Pacers, Brooklyn Nets, and Minnesota Timberwolves.

item.title: Shams: LeBron James wants to go somewhere he can compete for a championship and find happiness
item.description: Heat Culture: Shams on LeBron James: “It’s decision time for LeBron James… The Leading suitors have been CLE, MIA, GSW, PHI, and MIN…. LeBron James has made it clear privately that he wants to go somewhere he can compete for a championship. Somewhere he feels he finds that happiness from a team environment and CULTURE that he can uplift”

item.title: A third (and bow-tying) marriage with the Cleveland …
item.description: The sentiment I continue to hear from various teams chasing James has not really changed from the way Jake Fischer and I co-reported it below on July 4: A third (and bow-tying) marriage with the Cleveland Cavaliers continues to be regarded as “the scenario to beat” by the the teams pursuing James that have been most frequently mentioned: Golden State, Miami, Philadelphia, Minnesota and Denver. To beat. Not necessarily unbeatable.

Kalamian was an assistant coach for the Bucks, Pistons, Kings, Clippers, Raptors, Thunder, Timberwolves, and Nuggets. Herscu was Portland’s G League head coach and assistant coach for the Blazers and Kings. Ahearn was a Grizzlies assistant coach and San Antonio’s G League head coach.

item.title: “Obviously, he’s an unbelievable player. Whatever …

Whatever decision he makes is going to be the right one. There’s a lot going on. When you’re as great as he is, you should have optiona

item.title: "This is supposed to be fun," Connelly said. "He …
item.description: The flip side of Ball's penchant for undisciplined or flashy plays is a carefree style the Wolves could use. One of the undercurrents of their underachieving season that ended with a second-round loss to San Antonio was a moodiness that became part of their collective personality. Too many players took too long to shake off unproductive games or frustrating stretches. "This is supposed to be fun," Connelly said. "He enjoys life. He enjoys playing. You don't want to make this anything but where i

item.title: “I feel like it’s God’s plan,” a soft-spoken Ball said …
item.description: Ball had been the face of the Hornets franchise from the day he was drafted. He beat out Anthony Edwards for Rookie of the Year honors in 2021 and was an All-Star in his second season in the league. And he was coming off one of his best seasons, playing a central role in the Hornets’ stunning run over the second half of last season, a 33-15 finish that pushed them into the Play-In Tournament. Then, just when things finally appeared to be turning for the Hornets, Ball was sent away. “I feel like

item.title: YouTube leading candidate to house NBA's local broadcast streaming platform?
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said Tuesday night he expects the league’s aggregated streaming hub for local broadcasts to launch in time for the 2027-28 season, with sources telling SBJ that YouTube is a leading candidate to house the platform. Following the collapse of Main Street Sports Group, 13 teams -- the Hawks, Hornets, Cavaliers, Pistons, Pacers, Clippers, Grizzlies, Heat, Bucks, T’Wolves, Thunder, Magic and Spurs -- will sign what amounts to one-year deals this coming season with a varie

item.title: The Wolves want Anthony Edwards a year from now to …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "The idea is to get Anthony Edwards a year from now to put pen to paper on another monster extension. The idea is to elevate Ant's game, get him to be All-NBA once again, then he can maximize his earning potential. The Wolves want Anthony Edwards a year from now to sign that monster extension. LaMelo Ball, the thought is, is going to help elevate Anthony Edwards that much more."

item.title: "I know from Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez down to Tim …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "I know from Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez down to Tim Connelly, to Chris Finch, to Pablo Prigioni, Elston Turner, others on the coaching staff, other guys, that the Wolves feel like they have a really good infrastructure in place to help LaMelo mature that much more. And there is a thought from the deep dive that they've done, that LaMelo took a big step forward last year, certainly on the court. We can have the conversation about the car accident off the court, but on the court,

item.title: Timberwolves had talks with Boston about Jaylen Brown, Milwaukee about Giannis Antetokounmpo, Chicago about Josh Giddey
item.description: The Timberwolves had talks with the Celtics about Jaylen Brown, the Milwaukee Bucks about Giannis Antetokounmpo and the Chicago Bulls about Josh Giddey, according to team sources who granted anonymity so they could talk about sensitive negotiations. Brown was intriguing, but the Celtics were asking for a king’s ransom that included Gobert and heavy draft capital. There were also debates about how Edwards and Brown would fit together as two ISO-heavy scorers on offense. Connelly had given chase t

item.description: Etienne Catalan: Terrence Shannon Jr. (@Sn1per_T) will wear No. 4 for the #Timberwolves. Number last worn by Julian Phillips in 2026. #NBA

item.title: “My situation was totally different. I don’t know what …
item.description: Smith, whose historic five-year, $86 million contract with the Minnesota Timberwolves was voided by the league in 2000 due to an illegal, undisclosed “under-the-table” agreement, remains the poster child for NBA front-office policing. Yet, decades later, Smith is quick to point out that the narrative surrounding his past has long been stripped of critical context. “My situation was totally different. I don’t know what type of backstory these guys are getting from their agents, but mine was compl

item.title: “I was basically forced to play somewhere else,” Smith …
item.description: When NBA Commissioner David Stern handed down the hammer in 2000, the Timberwolves were fined $3.5 million and stripped of five consecutive first-round draft picks (though one was later restored). For Smith, the immediate professional fallout was jarring. “I was basically forced to play somewhere else,” Smith recalled. “When the ruling came down, I had two options: if I wanted to go back to Minnesota, I had to sit out an entire year before I could sign a contract with them again. Or, I could sig

item.title: “Even when I eventually went back to Minnesota after …
item.description: Smith ultimately signed a one-year, $2.25 million deal with the Detroit Pistons for the 2000-01 season before returning to Minneapolis. Still, the shadow of the scandal followed him for the rest of his career. “Even when I eventually went back to Minnesota after the fact, it was something I had to talk about all the time,” Smith said. “I had to hear about it because of the draft picks the Timberwolves lost. I think I took more of the brunt of that hit than anybody. And it was much more my agent’

item.title: “They telling you, Ant Edwards never reached out to …
item.description: In my exclusive deep dive earlier this week detailing The Architect vs. The Accelerant, I revealed that a potential pairing in the Twin Cities was being heavily bottlenecked because Minnesota Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards had not yet extended a direct, formal line of communication to recruit James. That report immediately set the league’s baseline for Minnesota’s true pursuit. However, high-level sources intimately familiar with James’ camp have immediately moved to clear the air, reaching o

item.description: Kalan Hooks: Anthony Edwards revealed he and Karl-Anthony Towns spoke before every game in the NBA Finals. He gave KAT tips on guarding the Spurs in the Finals.

item.title: “I told them we need to take a deep breath,” Bubes …
item.description: Nathan Bubes walked into the makeshift locker room at halftime of the Minnesota Timberwolves’ Las Vegas Summer League opener against New Orleans with a message. “I told them we need to take a deep breath,” Bubes said. “And as I said that, I was like, ‘So do I.'” Summer league is a chance for players with a lower profile to get noticed and try to earn a spot on a roster, either as a full-time or two-way player. It also offers that opportunity to young coaches, who are typically entrusted with run

item.title: “He deserves the opportunity just because of how …
item.description: Bubes has long been the right-hand man for Wolves head coach Chris Finch, winning him over when both were in New Orleans and then serving as his chief of staff in Minnesota. He’s done everything asked of him, including serving as the man on the hot seat during games to determine if Finch should challenge a call, spending a season as an assistant with the G League team in Iowa and easing Finch’s day-to-day operations by helping with planning, organizing and communicating with the rest of the staf

item.title: Anthony Edwards says LaMelo Ball has been staying at …

item.title: The Lakers’ 2025-26 media guide lists just three …
item.description: Two candidates, Minnesota’s Steve Senior and Philadelphia’s Prosper Karangwa, are widely believed to have turned the Lakers down before staying with their current organizations. Adding the head of scouting is seen as the first step for the Lakers to address a longtime need: a lack of a robust pro scouting department. The Lakers’ 2025-26 media guide lists just three full-time domestic scouts and two international scouts. All, per league sources, had been primarily (if not fully) tasked with amate

item.title: Anthony Edwards on why he isn't one of the faces of the league: I haven't won a championship yet
item.description: NBA Courtside: Anthony Edwards doesn’t believe he is one of the faces of the league: “I think the face of the league comes with a lot of responsibilities and I think one of those is winning a championship. I haven’t did that yet. Maybe Jalen Brunson or one of those guys” (Via @Fanatics)

item.title: “It’s definitely going to be ups and downs. First year …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Anthony Edwards on playing with LaMelo Ball: “It’s definitely going to be ups and downs. First year together maybe a lot of downs. We’ll celebrate both of them. Celebrate the ups, celebrate the downs.” (Via @Fanatics)

item.title: Paris Basketball are nearing a deal to appoint …
item.description: Paris Basketball are nearing a deal to appoint Minnesota Timberwolves assistant Max Lefevre as their new head coach, according to BasketNews sources. The two sides are nearing an agreement that would bring the French coach back to Europe and hand him his first permanent head-coaching position at the senior professional level. Lefevre is expected to replace Julius Thomas, who left Paris after finishing the 2025-26 season as the club's interim head coach. Thomas guided the team to another appearan

item.title: NBA star Anthony Edwards scored a victory in his …
item.description: NBA star Anthony Edwards scored a victory in his never-ending battle with his baby mama, Ayesha Howard, after a Georgia judge ruled on child support for their 1-year-old daughter, TMZ has learned. According to court docs obtained by TMZ, a Georgia judge ordered the Minnesota Timberwolves star to pay $3,222 a month in child support.

item.title: United. Anytime you guys spend money with any people …
item.description: Jaylen Clark: The another thing that we have is called an NBA licensing check. So, every time you guys play 2K, buy cards for like from Fanatics. We have a a new clothing brand for the NBPA that's coming out called Players United. Anytime you guys spend money with any people that are sponsored with the NBA, we get paid for that. So, this year the licensing check this year was $385,000. That's just for like I said 2K spending money with on cards, jerseys, like everything. So instead of like basic

item.title: According to “Mozzart Sport”, Wendell Moore Jr. is on …
item.description: After the official confirmations of the arrivals of Kevarrius Hayes, Lamar Stevens, and then Alessandro Pajola, the black-and-whites are close to signing another player. According to “Mozzart Sport”, Wendell Moore Jr. is on the verge of joining Partizan, and it seems that they will win the race for the American’s signature against Hapoel Tel Aviv. item.title: “Sources tell me LeBron James, his agent Rich Paul, …
item.description: Heat Central: “Sources tell me LeBron James, his agent Rich Paul, they’re not waiting for another domino right now. They’re not waiting for Anthony Davis to get traded. CLE, MIA, GSW, PHI, MIN, they’re set. Those are the teams. This is going to be based on happiness, chance to contend for a championship, join a culture”
